topic: Create and configure an Azure Backup vault
key_facts: The security features ensure you can secure your backups, and safely recover data, even if production and backup servers are compromised.
Azure provides various built-in roles , and Azure Backup has three built-in roles to manage recovery points .
By default, all your data is encrypted using Microsoft managed keys.
This includes access to Azure resources such as Azure Disks or PostgreSQL servers to be backed up and Key Vaults used for encryption key management.
Specific roles can be assigned to these identities to grant the required access.

Backup vaults support two types of managed identities:

 
 System Assigned Managed Identity: This identity is automatically created when the Backup vault is provisioned and is tied to the lifecycle of the vault.
These identities ensure secure and manageable access control, enabling Backup vaults to operate with the least privilege necessary while aligning with organizational security policies.
